patterns.name: inna — the certainty opener
patterns.what: إِنَّ starts a sentence with force: 'indeed / truly'. It's everywhere in the Quran. Practical rule: after إِنَّ the noun takes -a (accusative): إِنَّ اللهَ، إِنَّ الإِنسَانَ. With pronouns it fuses: إِنَّهُ (indeed he), إِنَّا (indeed we), إِنَّكَ (indeed you).

examples.en: Indeed Allah is Forgiving, Merciful.
Indeed mankind is in loss. (103:2)
Indeed We gave you al-Kawthar. (108:1)

test.prompt: '___ Allah is with the patient.' — write the opener

patterns.name: alladhina — 'those who'
patterns.what: الَّذِي (who, m.), الَّتِي (who, f.), الَّذِينَ (those who) glue a description onto a noun. The Quran's most famous phrase: الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا 'those who believe'. Hear الذين and expect a description of a group to follow.

examples.en: Those who believe and do righteous deeds. (103:3)
The path of those You have blessed. (1:7)

examples.ar: ذَٰلِكَ الْكِتَابُ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ هُدًى لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ
examples.en: …a guidance for those mindful of Allah. (2:2)

test.prompt: Complete the famous phrase: الَّذِينَ ___ (they believed)

patterns.name: idafa — possession by pairing
patterns.what: Arabic says 'the Lord of the worlds' by just putting two nouns together: رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ. No word for 'of'! First noun never takes ال; the second one usually does. You already know: يَوْمِ الدِّينِ (Day of Judgment), بَيْتِ اللهِ (house of Allah).

examples.en: Lord of the worlds. (1:2)
Master of the Day of Judgment. (1:4)
the people of the house

test.prompt: 'the Day of Judgment' (1:4, two words after مالك)

patterns.name: attached pronouns — my, your, his, their
patterns.what: Arabic attaches its pronouns to the END of words: ـِي my (رَبِّي my Lord), ـكَ your (رَبُّكَ your Lord), ـهُ his/him (لَهُ to him), ـهُمْ their/them (عَلَيْهِمْ upon them), ـنَا our/us (اهْدِنَا guide us). Master these five endings and huge amounts of Quran unlock.

examples.en: So pray to YOUR Lord. (108:2)
Guide US to the straight path. (1:6)
You have blessed THEM. (1:7)

patterns.name: past vs present — the two verb shapes
patterns.what: Past: the root alone — قَالَ he said, خَلَقَ he created, عَلِمَ he knew. Present: add يَـ for 'he' — يَقُولُ he says, يَخْلُقُ he creates, يَعْلَمُ he knows. For 'they' in the past add ـُوا: قَالُوا they said, آمَنُوا they believed. Recognize these two shapes and you can tell WHEN everything happens.

examples.en: your Lord SAID / those who SAY
He CREATED man. (96:2)
they BELIEVED and they DID (103:3)

patterns.name: the four ways to say 'not'
patterns.what: لَا + present = doesn't/don't (لَا يَعْلَمُونَ they don't know). مَا + past = didn't (مَا كَانَ he was not). لَمْ + short present = didn't, emphatic (لَمْ يَلِدْ He did not beget). لَنْ + present = will never (لَن تَفْعَلُوا you will never do). When you hear any of these, the sentence flips negative.

examples.en: He did not beget nor was He born. (112:3)
There is no doubt in it. (2:2)
The Day when wealth will not benefit. (26:88)

test.prompt: Which particle negates the PAST with a normal verb? ('he did not...')

patterns.name: the connectors — and, so, then
patterns.what: وَ = and (glued to the next word: وَعَمِلُوا 'and they did'). فَـ = so/then, immediate result (فَصَلِّ 'so pray'). ثُمَّ = then, after a while. Recitation flows on these three — hearing them tells you how ideas link.

examples.en: SO pray to your Lord AND sacrifice. (108:2)
they believed AND did righteous deeds. (103:3)
THEN I drink coffee. (your story!)

patterns.name: preposition + pronoun fusions
patterns.what: Prepositions fuse with pronouns constantly: لِـ+هُ = لَهُ (to him/His), فِي+هِ = فِيهِ (in it), مِنْ+هُ = مِنْهُ (from it), بِـ+هِ = بِهِ (by it), عَلَى+هِ = عَلَيْهِ (upon him), لَـ+هُمْ = لَهُمْ (for them). These tiny fused words carry the Quran's sentences — learn to hear them as units.

examples.en: no doubt IN IT. (2:2)
and there is none comparable TO HIM. (112:4)
FOR THEM is a great reward.
